hintDisplay: Sujeto: los pacientes / Verbo: comer (condicional pasado) / Complemento: comida más saludable / Verbo: desarrollar (condicional pasado, negado) / Complemento: diabetes
spanishSentence: Si los pacientes hubieran comido comida más saludable, no habrían desarrollado diabetes.
correctSentence: If the patients had eaten healthier food, they would not have developed diabetes.

hints: Complemento: hacia mejores resultados de salud mental

spanishSentence: Si hubieras seguido mi orientación, te habría guiado hacia mejores resultados de salud mental.
correctSentence: If you had followed my guidance, I would have guided you toward better mental health outcomes.

hints: Complemento: al doctor sobre sus alergias

spanishSentence: Si el paciente le hubiera informado al doctor sobre sus alergias, el doctor no habría prescrito ese medicamento.
correctSentence: If the patient had informed the doctor about their allergies, the doctor would not have prescribed that medication.

hints: Tiempo: Condicional pasado (si algo hubiera pasado en el pasado)

hintDisplay: Tiempo: Condicional pasado (si algo hubiera pasado en el pasado) / Sujeto: tú / Verbo: visitar (condicional perfecto - acción hipotética pasada) / Complemento: al dentista regularmente / Sujeto: él (el dentista) / Verbo: obturar (condicional perfecto - resultado hipotético) / Complemento: tu caries / Complemento: antes de que se volviera dolorosa (cláusula temporal)
spanishSentence: Si hubieras visitado al dentista regularmente, él habría obturado tu caries antes de que se volviera dolorosa.
correctSentence: If you had visited the dentist regularly, he would have filled your cavity before it became painful.

hints: Complemento: mucho más rápido de nuestra enfermedad

correctSentence: If we had followed the doctor's advice, we would have recovered much faster from our illness.

correctSentence: If I had eaten healthier food, I would not have developed diabetes.

correctSentence: If I had exercised regularly, I would not have developed heart problems.

correctSentence: If the nurse had not measured the patient's blood pressure, we would not have detected the high hypertension.

correctSentence: If the patient had come to the clinic earlier, the doctor would have diagnosed the disease at an earlier stage.

correctSentence: If the patients had rested properly, they would not have developed complications from their illness.

correctSentence: If I had exercised regularly, I would not have developed health problems.

hintDisplay: Sujeto: yo / Verbo: descansar (condicional perfecto negativo) / Complemento: adecuadamente / Verbo: recuperarse (condicional perfecto negativo) / Complemento: de mi enfermedad

correctSentence: If I had not rested properly, I would not have recovered from my illness.

hintDisplay: Sujeto: el paciente / Verbo: llegar (pretérito pluscuamperfecto del subjuntivo) / Complemento: más temprano / Sujeto: la enfermera / Verbo: administrar (condicional perfecto) / Complemento: la medicina / Complemento: más efectivamente

correctSentence: If the patient had arrived earlier, the nurse would have administered the medication more effectively.

correctSentence: If the therapist had not evaluated the patient's mental health carefully, the treatment would not have been effective.

correctSentence: If we had not eaten unhealthy food, we would not have developed these health problems.

correctSentence: If you had brushed your teeth regularly, the dentist would have cleaned them more easily during your last appointment.

correctSentence: If I had walked every day, I would have been healthier.

correctSentence: If they had followed the doctor's advice, they would not have developed serious health complications.

correctSentence: If the patient had not taken the medication, the infection would have gotten worse.

Verbo de consecuencia: blanquear (en condicional perfecto)

spanishSentence: Si hubieras cepillado tus dientes regularmente, el dentista los habría blanqueado más efectivamente.
correctSentence: If you had brushed your teeth regularly, the dentist would have whitened them more effectively.
